summary: Tested chained dropdown filtering in Add Car form - all tests passed. Engine and package dropdowns now correctly show model-specific options instead of all 100+ options.

issues: HTML span cannot be child of option/select warnings (cosmetic React hydration warning, not impacting functionality)

passed_tests: BMW 3 Serisi shows exactly 6 model-specific engines: 2.0, 2.0 TDI, 3.0, 3.0 TDI, Plug-in Hibrit, Mild Hibrit
BMW 3 Serisi shows exactly 7 model-specific packages: Base, Advantage, Sport Line, Luxury Line, M Sport, M Sport Pro, Individual
Fiat Egea shows exactly 4 model-specific engines: 1.3 MultiJet, 1.4, 1.4 Fire, 1.6 MultiJet
Tesla Model 3 shows exactly 2 electric motor options: Elektrik (Single Motor), Elektrik (Dual Motor)
Brand change resets model, engine, and package selections to empty
Model change resets engine and package selections to empty
Models without specific engine data (BMW 6 Serisi) fallback to full 135 engine list
Models without specific package data fallback to brand-level packages or general list
Dashboard loads correctly with car stock data visible
Add Car modal opens correctly via quick-add-car button
context_for_next_testing_agent: Chained dropdown fix verified. carData.js has modelEngineTypes and modelPackageTypes objects for model-specific filtering. getEnginesForModel(brand, model) returns model-specific engines or null (full list fallback). getPackagesForModel(brand, model) returns model-specific -> brand-level -> general fallback chain. AddCarModal.jsx brand change handler resets model, engine_type, package_info. Model change handler resets engine_type, package_info.

chained_dropdown_filtering.brand_change_behavior: Resets model, engine_type, package_info to empty
chained_dropdown_filtering.engine_fallback: null from getEnginesForModel -> full engineTypes list (135 options)

mocked_apis.Email verification: MOCKED - verification code shown on screen
